name: cable seated high row (v-bar)
instructions: Sit on the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing each other, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing each other, and your hands shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips. Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles. Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
